mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates weakening aggregate demand, as reduced construction activity and tighter lending conditions jointly suppress household spending and corporate revenues.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% MoM while RSAFS declines > 3% MoM
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a late-cycle expansion triggers inventory accumulation and margin compression, as firms outpace demand and face rising input costs, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: High INDPRO growth periods consistently yield positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential acceleration in quarterly real GDP growth confirms expanding economic activity, which directly supports upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration periods produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the Federal Reserve's implicit target triggers market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises the equity risk premium and discount rates, depressing forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns, or the signal fails to reject the null of zero predictive power in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage stability without triggering recessionary dynamics, fostering a favorable earnings environment and positive short-term equity performance.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k MoM while UNRATE falls
failure_condition: The joint condition predicts negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts following a credit or economic shock signals the initiation of a construction-driven recovery cycle, which multiplies through related industries and generates positive medium-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts surges fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the predictive signal reverses direction during high-volatility regimes.
